title: What to know about Centella asiatica before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Centella asiatica, commonly known as gotu kola, is a traditional herbal remedy with a range of medicinal uses. It is used to treat conditions such as anxiety, depression, and stress, as well as to improve cognitive function and memory. The herb's main health benefits include improved wound healing, reduced inflammation, and enhanced skin health. Its therapeutic properties include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects. The bioactive compounds triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids are found in Centella asiatica, which is often used to make teas, infusions, and supplements.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the rhizomes of Zingiber officinale have been found to exhibit several health benefits. The extract from these rhizomes has been shown to reduce inflammation and fever in rats, as well as inhibit the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Additionally, it has been demonstrated to lower blood glucose levels in rabbits and exhibit a dose-dependent inhibition of prostaglandin release, suggesting its potential in managing pain and other conditions.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Centella asiatica are its efficacy in treating memory loss due to its content of asiaticoside, brahmoside, and madecassoside, which have been shown to enhance memory and cognitive function by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters. Centella asiatica also has a significant role in wound healing, attributed to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, which stimulate collagen synthesis and enhance the proliferation of fibroblasts, thereby promoting tissue repair. Furthermore, the plant's antioxidant and anxiolytic properties, largely due to the action of flavonoids and phenolic acids, make it an effective treatment for anxiety and stress-related disorders. Additionally, Centella asiatica has been used to address hair loss by stimulating hair growth and improving scalp health, thanks to the presence of madecassic acid and asiatic acid, which have been shown to enhance the activity of 5-alpha-reductase inhibitors. Centella asiatica has also been used to treat a range of conditions, including diabetes, skin conditions such as eczema, digestive issues, inflammation, and Alzheimer's disease, all of which are attributed to the plant's rich content of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

uses_list.description: It has been found that extracts from this plant have potential neuroprotective and anti-amyloidogenic effects, which may help to improve memory loss associated with neurodegenerative diseases.
The plant's extracts have been shown to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can promote wound healing and tissue repair.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anxiolytic effects, which may help to reduce anxiety and stress in individuals.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-androgenic effects, which may help to reduce hair loss associated with androgenetic alopecia.
The plant's extracts have been found to have hypoglycemic effects, which may help to reduce blood glucose levels in individuals with diabetes.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of eczema.
The plant's extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation in various conditions.
Its extracts have been found to have potential neuroprotective and anti-amyloidogenic effects, which may help to improve symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Centella asiatica is used to accelerate wound healing through its ability to enhance collagen synthesis and tissue repair. Additionally, Centella asiatica has been shown to effectively reduce anxiety symptoms by modulating the activity of GABA receptors, a key neurotransmitter involved in stress regulation. Research also indicates that Centella asiatica may have a potential therapeutic role in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease, possibly due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Furthermore, studies have demonstrated the efficacy of Centella asiatica in managing diabetes, primarily through its ability to improve insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in cells. The plant's anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties also make it a promising treatment for conditions such as memory loss, hypertension, Parkinson's disease, and trauma-induced inflammation.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss, which is attributed to its ability to improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain. The plant is also utilized to alleviate anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing stress. Centella asiatica has been found to be beneficial in managing diabetes by regulating blood sugar levels and enhancing insulin sensitivity. Additionally, it is used to address inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in various diseases, by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting anti-inflammatory responses. Centella asiatica is also employed to treat hypertension, digestive issues, wound healing, skin ulcers, dementia, and gastrointestinal issues, highlighting its versatility in addressing a range of health concerns.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss by improving cognitive function due to its triterpenoid saponins, which enhance memory and learning. It is also valued for its wound healing properties, where it accelerates tissue repair and reduces oxidative stress. Additionally, Centella asiatica has been used to alleviate skin conditions, including eczema and psoriasis, by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration. Furthermore, it is utilized to manage anxiety by modulating the neurotransmitter GABA and reducing stress hormones. Its medicinal properties also extend to the treatment of digestive issues, hair loss, diabetes, arthritis, inflammation, and cognitive decline, making it a versatile herb in ayurvedic medicine.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss by enhancing cognitive function and improving neural connections. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help with hair loss by promoting scalp health and increasing hair growth. Centella asiatica is also used to manage diabetes due to its ability to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Additionally, it is employed to alleviate anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation. This herb is further valued for its role in wound healing, eczema, skin conditions, digestive issues, and hemorrhoids, as well as its anticonvulsant properties in treating epilepsy.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Centella asiatica is used to treat memory loss due to its ability to improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain. It has also been used to alleviate anxiety by reducing stress and promoting relaxation through its adaptogenic properties. Additionally, Centella asiatica is known to accelerate wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis and improving tissue repair. Furthermore, it is often used to treat eczema and other skin conditions by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration. This versatile herb is also used to address hair loss, digestive issues, insomnia, diabetes, and inflammation, making it a valuable remedy in homeopathic medicine.
benefits_description: Centella asiatica improves cognitive function through its triterpenoid saponins, which enhance neural transmission and increase the expression of neurotrophic factors. Enhancing memory is also a benefit of Centella asiatica, thanks to its ability to promote the growth of new neurons and improve synaptic plasticity. Reducing anxiety is another effect of Centella asiatica, which has been shown to decrease the production of stress hormones like cortisol. Promoting neuroprotective effects, Centella asiatica has been found to reduce the toxicity of amyloid beta and prevent neuronal apoptosis in Alzheimer's disease models. Supporting skin health through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, Centella asiatica has been used to treat eczema and other skin conditions, while also improving wound healing and liver function by enhancing the production of liver enzymes and reducing inflammation, which in turn improves immune function by stimulating the production of cytokines and activating immune cells.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is attributed to the presence of compounds such as asiaticoside, brahmoside, and centelloside, which have been shown to enhance neuronal function and promote neuroprotection, leading to improved cognitive function and reduced risk of neurodegenerative diseases.

The plant's ability to enhance memory is due to its content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to increase the expression of genes involved in memory formation and consolidation, and to reduce the levels of amyloid beta, a protein associated with Alzheimer's disease.

The plant's anxiolytic properties are attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to interact with the GABA receptor and to reduce the levels of stress hormones such as cortisol, leading to a decrease in anxiety and stress levels.

The plant's neuroprotective properties are due to its content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to reduce the levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines and to increase the levels of anti-inflammatory cytokines, leading to a reduction in neuroinflammation and a promotion of neuronal survival.

The plant's ability to support skin health is attributed to its content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and enhance collagen synthesis, leading to improved skin elasticity and reduced signs of aging.

The plant's ability to improve wound healing is due to its content of triterpenoid saponins, which have been found to promote tissue repair, reduce inflammation, and enhance collagen synthesis, leading to faster wound closure and reduced scarring.

The plant's ability to enhance liver health is attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to reduce liver inflammation, enhance antioxidant defenses, and promote the excretion of toxins, leading to improved liver function and reduced risk of liver disease.

The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are due to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to reduce the levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines and to increase the levels of anti-inflammatory cytokines, leading to a reduction in inflammation and a promotion of tissue repair.

The plant's ability to improve immune function is attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to enhance the activity of immune cells such as natural killer cells and macrophages, and to promote the production of antibodies, leading to improved immune defenses and reduced risk of infection.

The plant's antioxidant properties are due to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to scavenge free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and promote the expression of antioxidant enzymes, leading to improved cellular protection and reduced risk of chronic diseases.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Centella asiatica improves blood flow by increasing nitric oxide production, which relaxes blood vessels and allows for greater blood volume. Enhancing circulation is also facilitated by the plant's antioxidant properties, which protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Reduced inflammation is a result of centella asiatica's anti-inflammatory activity, which decreases prostaglandin production and prevents tissue damage, supporting vein health and preventing the formation of blood clots. Boosting oxygen delivery to tissues and organs is achieved through the plant's ability to increase capillarization and improve microcirculation, strengthening heart function and regulating blood pressure by stimulating cardiac contractility and vasodilation. Soothing varicose veins, improving peripheral circulation, and enhancing cardiovascular health are also benefits of centella asiatica, which is attributed to its flavonoid and saponin content that exhibit vasoprotective and vasoconstrictive properties.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Centella asiatica reduces inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to alleviate digestive issues. The plant protects intestinal mucosa with its mucilaginous compounds, preventing damage from digestive enzymes and other irritants. It regulates gut motility through its action on the enteric nervous system, balancing the movement of food through the digestive tract and preventing diarrhea attacks. Additionally, Centella asiatica enhances digestive enzymes, allowing for more efficient nutrient absorption, and increases gut barrier integrity, reducing the permeability of the gut. The antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of Centella asiatica also support liver detox, boost probiotic growth, soothe irritable bowel syndrome, and improve nutrient absorption, making it a valuable herb for maintaining a healthy digestive system and supporting liver function.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Centella asiatica regulates hormonal balance through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with endocrine disorders. The plant's ability to boost insulin sensitivity and production is attributed to its hypoglycemic and insulinogenic effects, making it a potential treatment for diabetes. Centella asiatica also stabilizes adrenal function, thanks to its adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties, which mitigate the negative effects of cortisol on the body. Furthermore, Centella asiatica supports thyroid function by enhancing the conversion of T4 to T3 and reducing thyroid peroxidase antibodies, while also releasing growth factors that promote tissue repair and regeneration. Additionally, Centella asiatica protects the pancreas from oxidative damage, balances cortisol levels by reducing cortisol-induced inflammation, enhances ovarian function by promoting follicular growth, improves metabolic function by increasing glucose uptake in muscles, and reduces stress by modulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Centella asiatica heals skin lesions due to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogens and prevent infection. It repairs damaged skin by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving tissue regeneration. Centella asiatica protects skin cells from oxidative stress by exhibiting potent antioxidant activity, which also enhances skin elasticity by increasing hyaluronic acid production. The plant reduces skin inflammation by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and improves skin regeneration by promoting the proliferation of fibroblasts and keratinocytes. Additionally, Centella asiatica prevents skin aging by scavenging free radicals and accelerating wound healing by enhancing blood flow and promoting tissue repair.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Centella asiatica boosts lymph flow through its triterpenoid saponins, which stimulate lymphatic vessels to contract and dilate, increasing lymph circulation. This plant enhances detoxification by containing antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, thereby facilitating the removal of toxins from the body. Centella asiatica improves lymphatic drainage by possessing anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and promote the breakdown of protein-rich fluids, enabling the efficient removal of waste products. It reduces swelling quickly due to its vasodilatory effect, which increases blood flow and facilitates the removal of excess fluids. By exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, Centella asiatica supports immune function, fosters inflammation reduction, promotes cell regeneration, encourages waste removal, regulates lymphatic pressure, and stimulates collagen production, ultimately contributing to a healthy lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Centella asiatica reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are essential for preventing further damage to the musculoskeletal system. Its triterpene compounds, particularly asiatic acid and madecassic acid, are responsible for relaxing muscles, thereby reducing muscle spasms and promoting a state of relaxation. The same triterpene compounds also improve flexibility by increasing the production of hyaluronic acid, a key component of synovial fluid, which lubricates joints and tendons. Centella asiatica enhances mobility by stabilizing joints, thanks to its saponin content, which strengthens connective tissue and prevents joint degeneration. The plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also soothe joints, protect tendons, heal ligaments, reduce pain, and improve balance, ultimately contributing to the overall health and well-being of the musculoskeletal system.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Centella asiatica improves cognitive function by enhancing neuroplasticity and protecting brain cells from oxidative stress, which is attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This plant also enhances neuroplasticity by increasing the expression of neurotrophic factors, such as BDNF, which promotes neural adaptation and learning. Furthermore, Centella asiatica reduces anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin, and its anti-inflammatory properties help to regulate mood by reducing inflammation in the brain. The plant's antimicrobial and anti-oxidant properties also enable it to soothe nerves and relax muscles by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Additionally, Centella asiatica boosts memory and improves concentration by promoting the growth of new neurons and improving neural communication, while stabilizing emotions by regulating the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Centella asiatica regulates hormones by possessing adaptogenic properties, which help to normalize the body's response to stress and maintain homeostasis. This plant boosts fertility by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, thereby protecting the reproductive system from oxidative stress and inflammation. Centella asiatica improves ovulation by containing bioactive compounds that stimulate the release of gonadotropin-releasing hormone, which in turn regulates the menstrual cycle and promotes ovulation. It enhances libido by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and infections in the reproductive tract, thereby increasing sexual desire. Centella asiatica supports pregnancy by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the risk of complications during pregnancy and promote a healthy fetal environment, and also reduces infertility by stimulating the growth of new ovarian follicles, improves erectile function by exhibiting vasodilatory properties, promotes conception by containing bioactive compounds that stimulate the release of hormones involved in fertilization, and increases ovum by exhibiting anti-apoptotic properties, which help to prevent the death of oocytes.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Centella asiatica reduce inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. This reduction in inflammation is also associated with a boost in lung function, as the plant's flavonoids and triterpenoids improve the elasticity of lung tissue and enhance gas exchange. The strengthening of the respiratory system is a direct result of the plant's antimicrobial properties, which prevent infections and promote a healthy respiratory environment. Additionally, Centella asiatica alleviate bronchitis and relieve asthma by modulating the immune response and reducing airway inflammation, ultimately improving oxygenation and enhancing mucus clearance. By soothing coughing and promoting exhalation, Centella asiatica prevent respiratory complications and support a healthy respiratory system.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Centella asiatica protect bladder by its antimicrobial properties, reducing bacterial growth and preventing infections in the urinary system. Reducing incontinence is also a benefit of Centella asiatica, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urinary tract muscles. Lower blood pressure is another effect of Centella asiatica, attributed to its antioxidant properties that help regulate blood flow and improve urination. Centella asiatica also boost immunity by stimulating the immune system, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe inflammation, preventing further damage to the urinary system. By regulating hormones and enhancing detox, Centella asiatica strengthen muscles in the urinary system, including the bladder and pelvic floor muscles, preventing further damage and improving overall urinary function.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Centella asiatica are attributed to its antimicrobial effects, which are primarily due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids that inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. Centella asiatica exhibits antioxidant properties, which are mainly attributed to the bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Its anti-inflammatory properties are associated with the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, mediated by compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid. Additionally, the neuroprotective effects of Centella asiatica are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins that protect against neurodegenerative diseases by inhibiting amyloid-beta aggregation and oxidative stress. Centella asiatica's wound healing, antiseptic, antiviral, analgesic, hepatoprotective, and cardioprotective properties are also attributed to its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins, that promote tissue repair, inhibit pathogen growth, and protect against liver and heart damage.

properties_list.description: Centella asiatica has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, which can cause infections.

Centella asiatica contains antioxidants like asiatic acid, which has been found to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health.

The extracts of Centella asiatica have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions associated with inflammation, such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.

Centella asiatica has been studied for its potential neuroprotective effects, which may help in preventing or treating neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's and Parkinson's by protecting neurons from damage and promoting their growth.
The extracts of Centella asiatica have been found to promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and accelerating the process of wound closure.

Centella asiatica has been traditionally used as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections, and its extracts have been found to exhibit antiseptic properties, which can help prevent the growth of microorganisms in wounds.
Some studies have shown that Centella asiatica exhibits antiviral activity, inhibiting the replication of certain viruses and potentially providing protection against viral infections.
Centella asiatica has been found to possess analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, providing relief from conditions associated with pain.

The extracts of Centella asiatica have been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage caused by toxins and potentially alleviate conditions associated with liver disease.

Centella asiatica has been studied for its potential cardioprotective effects, which may help in preventing or treating cardiovascular diseases by reducing inflammation, improving lipid profiles, and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

constituents.description: Flavonoids found in Centella asiatica are antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and exhibit free radical scavenging activity.
A type of triterpenoid glycoside with surface-active properties, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hemolytic effects.
Phenolic acids in Centella asiatica have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, as well as the ability to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress.
Madecassoside is a triterpene saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.
A type of compound found in Centella asiatica, Triterpenoids exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties.
Triterpene glycosides found in Centella asiatica, including brahmoside, brahminoside and asiaticoside, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and cytoprotective properties.
Asiaticoside is an alkaloid with anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory and vasoprotective properties.
Asiaticoside is a triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.
A triterpenoid compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
A triterpenoid saponin with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Centella asiatica are a combination of flavonoids, saponins, phenolic acids, madecassoside, and triterpenoids. These constituents have been isolated and identified due to their ability to exhibit potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. Flavonoids in Centella asiatica, specifically quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Saponins, particularly madecassoside and asiaticoside, have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. Triterpenoids and phenolic acids in Centella asiatica have also been found to exhibit antioxidant and neuroprotective effects.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Centella asiatica for medicinal purposes are leaves and roots. The leaves contain primarily triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, contributing to their wound-healing, anti-aging, and cognitive-enhancing effects. The roots, on the other hand, are rich in saponins, particularly asiatic and madecassic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory activities, making them useful in treating various health conditions, including arthritis and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive compounds in both leaves and roots contribute to their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating properties, which are valuable in preventing and treating various diseases. The combination of these bioactive compounds in both leaves and roots makes Centella asiatica a valuable medicinal plant in traditional and modern medicine.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, particularly brahmoside and asiaticoside, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing properties, primarily benefiting skin health, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting tissue repair and regeneration.

The roots of this plant contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoid saponins, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and hepatoprotective properties, mainly contributing to improved immune function, reduced oxidative stress, and enhanced liver health.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and cognitive impairment, and to improve memory and cognitive function in patients with traumatic brain injury and stroke.
preparations.overview: Centella asiatica tincture is a preparation commonly used to treat various conditions including memory loss, eczema, wound healing, anxiety, diabetes, psoriasis, hemorrhoids, varicose veins, inflammation, and skin ulcers. It's a widely used herbal remedy due to its effectiveness and availability. One of the common effects of Centella asiatica tincture is improved cognitive function and skin health. The preparation is typically made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant, which are rich in active compounds. Making Centella asiatica tincture requires some expertise and involves a moderate level of difficulty, as it requires precise extraction of the plant's active ingredients to achieve the desired potency.
recipe: Gather fresh Centella asiatica leaves and roots, then wash and dry them thoroughly.
Combine 1 part Centella asiatica leaves and 1 part roots in a clean glass jar to create a 1:1 ratio.
Fill the jar with 80-90% vodka, covering the Centella asiatica mixture completely, then seal the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate extraction.

After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, then label it as 1:1 Centella asiatica tincture (25-30% alcohol by volume), with a recommended adult dosage of 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat skin wounds, improve cognitive function, and reduce inflammation and anxiety associated with neurodegenerative diseases.
preparations.overview: Centella asiatica salve is a topical preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including wound healing, eczema, burns, psoriasis, bruises, skin irritation, scars, stretch marks, scarring, and acne. It's commonly used due to its effectiveness. One common effect of Centella asiatica salve is its ability to accelerate wound healing and reduce inflammation. The preparation is made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant. The process of making Centella asiatica salve is quite labor-intensive, requiring careful harvesting and processing of the plant material to extract its active compounds, which are then combined with a carrier oil to create the final product.
recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh Centella asiatica leaves and 1/2 cup of dried Centella asiatica roots.
Combine the leaves and roots in a large pot and cover them with 2 cups of water, then bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a large bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax is melted.
Combine the herbal infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture, then pour into small tin containers and let cool before applying 2-3 times a day as needed.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, depression, insomnia, memory loss, and skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
preparations.overview: Centella asiatica capsule is a widely used herbal preparation used to treat various health conditions, including memory loss, anxiety, hypertension, hair loss, stress, wound healing, diabetes, eczema, skin conditions, and insomnia. It's one of the most common herbal capsules used globally due to its effectiveness. The common effect of Centella asiatica capsule is its ability to improve cognitive function, reduce anxiety and stress levels, and promote wound healing. This preparation is made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant, which are rich in active compounds like triterpenoids and phenolic acids. The process of making Centella asiatica capsule is moderately difficult, requiring proper extraction and drying of the plant material to retain its bioactive properties.
recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried Centella asiatica leaves and 50 grams of dried roots.
Crush the dried leaves and roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the leaf powder and root powder in a 2:1 ratio to create the herbal blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal blend, which is equivalent to 1-2 capsules per day as recommended dosage.
Seal the capsules tightly and store them in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis, and is also used to enhance cognitive function and memory.
preparations.overview: Centella asiatica tea is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, memory loss, stress, eczema, insomnia, diabetes, hypertension, wound healing, inflammation, and digestive issues. It is one of the most widely consumed herbal teas, with a long history of use in many cultures. The common effect of Centella asiatica tea is improved cognitive function and reduced stress levels. This preparation is typically made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. Fortunately, preparing Centella asiatica tea is relatively simple, as it can be brewed by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water for a few minutes.
recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of fresh Centella asiatica leaves and 1/2 teaspoon of dried Centella asiatica roots.
Combine the leaves and roots in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the leaves and roots, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink 1-2 cups of Centella asiatica herbal tea per day, following the recommended dosage of 2-4 grams of dried herb per day.

preparations.description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat anal fissures, hemorrhoids, and other local inflammatory conditions.
preparations.overview: Centella asiatica suppository is used to treat anal fissures, hemorrhoids, proctitis, anal fistula, rectal prolapse, hemorrhoids, prostatitis, fistula, constipation and diarrhea. It's an uncommonly used preparation due to its specific application method and the need for a sterile environment. The suppository has a unique effect, reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair in the affected areas. It's typically made from the leaves and roots of the Centella asiatica plant, which are rich in triterpenoid saponins. The process of preparing the suppository is complex and requires expertise, as it involves extracting the active compounds from the plant material and mixing them with a base that can be inserted rectally.
recipe: Dry 50g of Centella asiatica leaves and 20g of roots under shade for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves and roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the powder with 20g of Vaseline to create a smooth paste.
Pour the paste into a suppository mold and refrigerate for 2 hours to harden.
Insert 1 suppository, dosage 1 per day, into the rectum for treatment of inflammation and wound healing.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Centella asiatica for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are commonly used to treat anxiety and stress due to their adaptogenic properties. Salves, on the other hand, are applied topically to soothe and accelerate wound healing, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Capsules are often used to administer the extract orally, where it can aid in the treatment of cognitive impairment and memory loss. Additionally, tea is prepared from the leaves of Centella asiatica and is traditionally consumed to promote digestion and reduce inflammation in the gut. A suppository form of the extract is also available, which is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Abdominal pain occurs due to gastrointestinal irritation caused by Centella asiatica.
Centella asiatica ingestion causes increased gastrointestinal motility leading to loose stool.
Some individuals exhibit symptoms such as hives, itching, and swelling after consuming Centella asiatica.
Centella asiatica consumption has been associated with dizziness due to its potential to affect blood pressure.
Centella asiatica extracts decrease mean arterial pressure in healthy adults.
Ingesting large amounts of Centella asiatica can cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to nausea.
Gastrointestinal irritation occurs due to saponins and other compounds in Centella asiatica.
Consuming large amounts of Centella asiatica can cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to vomiting.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Centella asiatica are allergic reactions, which can manifest as skin rashes, itching, and in severe cases, anaphylaxis. Gastrointestinal upset is also a possibility, with symptoms including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Cardiovascular issues may arise, such as hypertension, arrhythmias, and even heart palpitations. Kidney damage is another potential side effect, as excessive consumption of the plant can lead to nephrotoxicity. Liver toxicity, characterized by elevated liver enzymes and jaundice, is a rare but serious side effect that requires immediate medical attention.

precautions.precaution_description: Pregnant or breastfeeding women should consult a physician before using Centella asiatica due to lack of available data on its safety during these periods.
Pregnant women should avoid using Centella asiatica due to potential estrogenic and uterotonic effects.
Centella asiatica may pose a risk to breastfeeding infants, and its use is contraindicated by the WHO.

Centella asiatica may lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension due to its flavonoid and terpenoid constituents, which inhibit alpha-adrenergic receptors and calcium channels, thereby reducing vascular resistance.
Centella asiatica extracts may exacerbate kidney impairment due to high oxalate content.
Centella asiatica may exacerbate liver disease due to its coumarin content, which can inhibit liver function.
Centella asiatica should be used under the supervision of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications and exacerbation of underlying conditions.
Begin with a low dose of 400 mg or less daily to minimize potential adverse effects and interactions.
Dosage should be increased gradually to prevent adverse effects in patients taking Centella asiatica for extended periods.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Centella asiatica medicinally are to consult a physician, especially for individuals with pre-existing conditions, to determine pregnancy status and avoid in breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions. Before commencing treatment, it is essential to monitor blood pressure and adjust the dosage accordingly, as Centella asiatica may lower blood pressure. Patients with kidney disease should avoid using Centella asiatica, as it may exacerbate renal impairment, and those with liver disease should also avoid it, as it may increase liver enzymes. Use of Centella asiatica should be under medical supervision, and to minimize adverse effects, start with low doses and gradually increase dosages.
identification: Centella asiatica is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae, characterized by its small, greenish-white flowers and umbrella-like inflorescences. It has palmate leaves with 5-7 lobes, typically 2-5 cm in length, and a distinctive leaf shape. Native to tropical and subtropical regions, this plant is widely distributed in Asia, Africa, and Australia, typically growing in shallow water or on wet soil in areas such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It thrives in full sun to partial shade and can tolerate a wide range of water levels, making it adaptable to various environments. Centella asiatica can grow up to 30 cm in height and is often found in dense groups, forming a dense mat of foliage.
cultivation: Centella asiatica is a low-maintenance, herbaceous perennial plant that thrives in warm, humid climates and can grow up to 30 cm tall, typically spreading by stolons to form dense mats. It requires partial shade to full sun, with an optimal temperature range of 20-30°C, and high humidity, usually above 60%, to promote healthy growth. To cultivate this plant, it's essential to provide well-draining soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, and ensure adequate water supply, although it can tolerate some drought. Growth can be accelerated by maintaining high humidity, using mulch to retain moisture, and providing supplemental lighting, especially during the initial growth stage. Regular pruning and division can help control its spread and encourage healthy, robust growth.
history: Centella asiatica is an herb that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices in India and the Middle East. The plant, also known as Gotu Kola, has been employed to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and memory loss, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it has been used to promote wound healing and improve cognitive function. Historical records also suggest that it was used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis. The plant's bioactive compounds, including asiatic acid and brahmoside, have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic effects.
